preamble: The shift from [ɛ] to [ui] represents a significant movement in the vowel space, involving backing of the nucleus and diphthongization towards a high front glide. This can be found in systems with extreme vowel raising and retraction.

examples.note: Extreme backing and raising of the nucleus in a diphthongal shift.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
